description: Whole-board power on a Radxa Cubie A7A (A733), measured in-line with a RuiDeng TC66C USB meter, averaged over 30 s per state (~300 samples), CPU utilisation tracked to confirm attribution. The load is the 3-model face pipeline (BlazeFace + Attention Mesh + Blendshapes) run at a fixed inference rate. Marginal = over the 2.42 W idle.

npu_sweep.note: NPU board power stays nearly flat up to ~60 fps, then climbs.

findings: At a fixed 15 fps face-landmark workload the NPU adds ~0.11 W vs ~0.77 W for one Cortex-A76 -> ~7x less compute power for the same result, at matched output accuracy.
NPU board power is nearly flat from 5 to 60 fps (2.50 -> 2.59 W, +0.09 W): you can raise the inference rate almost for free up to ~60 fps.
During NPU inference the A76 stays at 1-3% utilisation (416-627 MHz), so the measured watts are the NPU's, not hidden CPU work.
The 6x Cortex-A55 config is no faster than 1x A55 (~11 fps): the MediaPipe pipeline is sequential and does not parallelise across the little cores.
